about.title: About Me
about.description: Full Stack Software Engineer specialized in FinTech and GovTech. I design and build scalable SaaS systems that process thousands of daily transactions, with a relentless focus on client ROI.

background.title: The Origin
background.text: My engineering journey began by facing the most critical problem in business digitalization in emerging markets: systems that fail exactly when they are needed most. Building Sago One from scratch forced me to master offline-first architectures, government fiscal compliance, and bank-grade security protocols — not as academic exercises, but as business imperatives with real financial consequences. Every architectural decision I make starts with a central question: what is the direct impact on the client's operational balance sheet?

current.title: Production Ecosystem
current.text: Sago One processes over 10,000 DGI-certified electronic invoices, operates a touch POS, manages legal payroll, and maintains 99.9% uptime with a PWA architecture that works without internet. Plenty Market integrates a logistics Partner Program with tax-free zones and real-time inventory sync. Both are technological assets protecting the financial interests of real businesses, every day, with latencies under 200ms.

philosophy.title: My Philosophy
philosophy.text: Clean code, scalable solutions, clear documentation

values.title: Engineering Pillars
values.subtitle: Principles guiding every architectural decision and every line of code

architecture.title: Architecture First
architecture.desc: The tech stack is a consequence of the architecture, not the other way around. Every project starts with an analysis of non-functional requirements: scalability, fault tolerance, security. This prevents technical debt that erodes client ROI.

security.title: Non-Negotiable Security
security.desc: In FinTech systems, security is not a layer added at the end. AES-256, robust authentication, and strict data validation are part of the initial design. Regulatory compliance is not optional: it is the product.

durability.title: Software as an Asset
durability.desc: Strict TypeScript, clear documentation, and modular architectures are the difference between a system that scales for years and one that accumulates technical debt. The software I deliver is a capital asset, not an operating expense.

communication.title: Value in Business Terms
communication.desc: CTOs and CFOs don't invest in lines of code; they invest in predictable financial outcomes. I translate every technical decision — latency, uptime, scalability — into its direct impact on revenue, operating costs, and business risk.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How accurate is the calculated budget?
faq.a1: The budget is an estimate based on the parameters you select and our experience with similar projects. The final cost may vary based on additional specific requirements that emerge during the detailed project analysis.
faq.q2: Does the budget include all listed services?
faq.a2: Yes, the base budget includes all features listed for each service type. Additional add-ons are added separately and are optional but recommended depending on the project type.
faq.q3: How long is the quote valid?
faq.a3: Generated quotes are valid for 30 days from the generation date. After this period, we recommend generating a new quote to reflect possible changes in market conditions.
faq.q4: Can I modify the project after starting?
faq.a4: Yes, projects are flexible and can be adjusted during development. However, significant scope changes may affect the budget and timeline. We work in an agile way to adapt to your needs while maintaining cost transparency.

sago_one.title: Sago One
sago_one.label: All-in-One ERP SaaS | POS & FinTech | Next.js 15
sago_one.subtitle: Enterprise ERP and Electronic Invoicing Platform
sago_one.description: All-in-One ERP SaaS that eliminates hours of redundant administrative work weekly for Panamanian businesses. Certifies invoices with the DGI, manages real-time inventory, and operates a touch POS — all under an offline-first architecture that guarantees operational continuity and uninterrupted revenue even without internet. Payroll and inventory automation directly reduces fixed costs and eliminates the risk of tax penalties from human transcription errors.
sago_one.challenge: The critical challenge: guarantee business continuity — and revenue — during connectivity outages, which in emerging markets represent direct sales losses. The Service Workers and background sync solution ensures every transaction is recorded and invoiced regardless of network stability, protecting business cash flow at its most vulnerable moment.

security.title: Bank-Grade Security
security.desc: AES-256 encryption for PAC/DGI credentials. Mitigates the risk of tax penalties and corporate data breaches that could represent five-figure losses.

database.title: Modular Ecosystem (ERP)
database.desc: DGI Invoicing, touch POS, real-time inventory, and legal payroll integrated. Eliminates data silos and reduces monthly accounting close time.

performance.title: 99.9% Uptime · <200ms
performance.desc: React Server Components for optimal rendering. Every guaranteed uptime minute protects revenue: no downtime, no lost sales, no penalties for missing invoices.

plenty_market.title: Plenty Market
plenty_market.label: International E-commerce | PWA | Complex Logistics
plenty_market.subtitle: PWA E-commerce with Partner Program and Inventory Management
plenty_market.description: Migration from static infrastructure to a high-performance e-commerce platform with a direct impact on conversion: TTI reduced to under 3 seconds, a critical factor given that each additional second of latency reduces conversion rates by 7%. Integrates a logistics Partner Program with tax-free zones and real-time inventory sync, enabling business models and revenue streams that the previous architecture made impossible.
plenty_market.challenge: The challenge was migrating a static site to a full e-commerce platform without degrading user experience or losing existing SEO positioning. The Next.js 14 solution with aggressive asset optimization achieved sub-3s load times, increasing user retention and reducing cart abandonment at critical conversion funnel stages.

state.title: Conversion Optimized
state.desc: Zustand for overhead-free cart management. Persistent state between sessions: less checkout friction, higher conversion rate, lower cart abandonment.

media.title: Performance = Revenue
media.desc: Cloudinary with automatic WebP/AVIF transformation reduces image load time by 60%. In e-commerce, speed is a direct revenue lever.

migration.title: Architecture That Scales
migration.desc: PostgreSQL (Neon) via Prisma ORM guarantees data integrity under massive volumes. Infrastructure ready for traffic peaks without degradation or over-provisioning costs.

facturas.label: Invoices Processed
facturas.desc: Electronic invoices issued through Sago One with DGI compliance.

uptime.label: Guaranteed Uptime
uptime.desc: Availability of production services with serverless architecture.

latency.label: Response Time
latency.desc: Average API latency in critical invoicing operations.

offline.label: Offline Capable
offline.desc: Full offline functionality thanks to Service Workers and PWA.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about my services and methodology.

items.question: What development services does Ángel Nereira offer?
items.answer: I specialize in the development of high-performance Full Stack web applications, SaaS (Software as a Service) platforms for FinTech, integration of electronic invoicing systems, and development of Offline-First PWAs (Progressive Web Apps).

items.question: Do you work with international companies?
items.answer: Yes, I offer software consulting and development services for global clients, with experience working with teams in the United States, Europe, and Latin America.

items.question: What is your main technology stack?
items.answer: My main stack focuses on the React and Next.js ecosystem, using TypeScript for type safety, Tailwind CSS for design, and PostgreSQL (with Prisma/Neon) for scalable databases.

items.question: Do you offer AI integration services?
items.answer: Yes, I develop solutions that integrate Artificial Intelligence models (LLMs like GPT-4, Claude, Gemini) into business workflows for automation and data analysis.
